title: 10 best herbal decoctions for altitude sickness
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for altitude sickness are natural remedies made by steeping herbs in hot water, which can provide relief from the symptoms of acute mountain sickness (AMS). These decoctions work by improving oxygen delivery to the body's cells and reducing inflammation. Examples of helpful herbal decoctions include ginkgo biloba, which improves blood flow and oxygenation, and Rhodiola rosea, which helps reduce stress and fatigue. By using these natural remedies, travelers can enjoy their mountain adventures without worrying about AMS, and local communities can continue to thrive in high-altitude regions with minimal risk of illness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: King of bitters decoctions helps with altitude sickness because it contains a unique blend of herbs that work in harmony to alleviate symptoms. The decoction's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and congestion caused by high elevation, while its expectorant qualities relieve coughing and shortness of breath. Additionally, the herbs' antioxidant effects help protect against oxidative stress and cellular damage triggered by altitude changes, promoting a smoother adjustment to new elevations. By supporting the body's natural response to altitude changes, King of bitters decoctions helps alleviate symptoms and promote acclimatization.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: King of Bitters decoction's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by high altitude conditions, which can alleviate symptoms such as headaches and fatigue.

Cardiovascular tonic effects: The cardiovascular tonic effects of King of Bitters decoction help to improve blood circulation and oxygen delivery to the body's tissues, which can help to mitigate the effects of hypoxia (oxygen deficiency) at high altitudes.

Digestive and anti-inflammatory properties: King of Bitters decoction's digestive and anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and protect the gut lining, reducing symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea that are common in altitude sickness, while also alleviating inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Andrographolide: A diterpenoid lactone, andrographolide is believed to help alleviate altitude sickness by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which are often exacerbated by high altitudes.

N-Acetyl Andrographolide (NAAG): A derivative of andrographolide, NAAG is thought to help mitigate altitude sickness by modulating the body's response to hypoxia (low oxygen levels) and promoting the production of erythropoietin, a hormone that stimulates red blood cell production.

Andrographiside: A sesquiterpene lactone, andrographiside has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of andrographolide, a key bioactive compound with anti-inflammatory and adaptogenic properties.
Roots: Used due to their andrographolide content and potential to reduce inflammation and improve oxygen delivery to the body at high altitudes.
Stems: Used due to their ability to modulate the body's response to high altitude, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, and potentially improving symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of fresh andrographis paniculata leaves and stems from mature plants.
Dry the harvested material in a well-ventilated area for 1-3 days to reduce moisture content.
Use a mortar and pestle to grind 10-20 grams of dried andrographis paniculata into a fine powder.
Combine 1-2 teaspoons of the andrographis paniculata powder with 1 cup of boiling water to make a decoction.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes before straining and serving the decoction immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree decoctions helps with altitude sickness because it contains compounds that help to dilate blood vessels, increasing oxygen flow to the body's tissues. The decoction also has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the brain and other organs, which can occur as a result of high altitudes. Additionally, maidenhair tree decoctions have been shown to stimulate the immune system, helping the body to adapt to the lower oxygen levels found at high elevations. By promoting healthy circulation and reducing inflammation, maidenhair tree decoctions can help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness, making it an effective natural remedy for those who venture to high altitudes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba decoctions help protect against oxidative stress caused by high-altitude conditions, which can lead to inflammation and tissue damage.

Circulatory stimulant properties: The decoction's ability to dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow helps to increase oxygen delivery to the body's tissues, reducing symptoms of altitude sickness such as headache, fatigue, and dizziness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginkgo biloba's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation and swelling in the lungs and other organs that can occur at high altitudes, which can lead to respiratory distress and other complications.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids (Quercetin and Kaempferol): These flavonoids have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by high altitude, thereby alleviating symptoms of altitude sickness.

Terpenoids (Ginkgolides and Bilobalide): These terpenoids have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by high altitude, and also improve blood flow to the brain, reducing the risk of altitude sickness.

Bilobalide: This triterpene has neuroprotective properties, which can help protect the brain from damage caused by high altitude, and also improve blood flow to the brain, reducing the risk of altitude sickness and its symptoms such as headaches and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to help reduce the symptoms of altitude sickness.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds have been used to treat various health conditions, including altitude sickness, due to their potential to improve blood circulation and reduce oxidative stress.
Roots: Ginkgo roots are thought to have adaptogenic properties, helping the body to adapt to high-altitude environments and reducing the severity of altitude sickness symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and flowers from a reputable source.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over the dried ginkgo biloba mixture in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Store the cooled decoction in a sealed container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Golden root decoctions helps with altitude sickness because they contain bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of high-altitude maladies. The decoction's adaptogenic properties help the body acclimate to changes in oxygen levels, reducing the severity of headaches, fatigue, and nausea associated with altitude sickness. Additionally, golden root's antioxidant activity helps combat oxidative stress caused by high-altitude environments, further enhancing its therapeutic effects.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Golden Root's adaptogenic properties help the body adapt to high-altitude conditions by regulating cortisol levels, reducing stress, and improving oxygen delivery to the tissues.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of Golden Root decoctions help mitigate oxidative stress caused by high altitude exposure, which can damage cells and tissues, by neutralizing free radicals and protecting against cell damage.

Vasodilatory effects: Golden Root's vasodilatory properties help improve blood flow to the body's tissues, including those in the lungs and brain, which is essential for efficient oxygenation and adaptation to high-altitude conditions.
remedy_constituents: Rosavin: This phenolic glycoside helps with altitude sickness by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can be triggered by high altitudes.
Salidroside: This phenolic glycoside exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in mitigating the symptoms of altitude sickness, such as fatigue and headaches.

Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic acid has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect the body from the oxidative stress and inflammation associated with high-altitude exposure.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Rhodiola rosea are commonly used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, such as rosavin and salidroside, which are believed to help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Rhodiola rhyzomes are also used to make decoctions for altitude sickness because they contain a high amount of rosavin and other compounds that may help adapt the body to high altitudes.
Leaves: Rhodiola leaves are used in some traditional remedies for altitude sickness due to their antioxidant properties and ability to help the body adapt to stress and high altitudes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 ounces of rhodiola rosea roots and stems from the plant.
Rinse the harvested rhodiola roots and stems with water to remove dirt.
Combine 1-2 ounces of rhodiola roots and stems with 2 cups of cold water in a pot.
Boil the mixture for 5-10 minutes to extract the active compounds from the rhodiola.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with altitude sickness because it can help to reduce inflammation in the body, which is a common symptom of high-altitude conditions. The anti-inflammatory compounds present in licorice root, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, can help to soothe and calm the body's tissues, reducing swelling and discomfort. Additionally, licorice decoctions may also help to alleviate symptoms of acute mountain sickness, including headaches and fatigue, by promoting healthy digestion and supporting respiratory function.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has been shown to help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are common underlying factors in altitude sickness, by inhibiting the production of inflammatory chemicals.
Isomaltose: This sugar molecule helps to thin out mucus and facilitate its clearance from the lungs, alleviating congestion and shortness of breath that can occur at high altitudes.

Licoricidin: This compound has been found to have a vasodilatory effect, helping to widen blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can help alleviate symptoms such as dizziness, lightheadedness, and fatigue often experienced during altitude sickness.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Glycyrrhiza glabra may help alleviate altitude sickness by reducing inflammation in the body, which can be triggered by high altitudes and low oxygen levels.

Flavonoids (such as liquiritin): Flavonoids in Glycyrrhiza glabra may help mitigate altitude sickness by exerting antioxidant effects, protecting the body from oxidative stress caused by high-altitude conditions.

Glycyrrhetic acid: Glycyrrhetic acid in Glycyrrhiza glabra may help alleviate altitude sickness by modulating the body's response to high altitude, possibly by regulating the levels of various hormones and reducing the severity of altitude-related symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make decoctions for altitude sickness because they contain glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make decoctions for altitude sickness because they have diuretic properties that can help reduce fluid buildup in the body caused by altitude.

Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make decoctions for altitude sickness because they contain flavonoids and saponins, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted supplier or grow your own.
Cut the roots into small pieces to increase the surface area for infusion.
Combine the root pieces with 1 liter of boiling water in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to dissolve.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with altitude sickness because they contain a unique combination of flavonoids, terpenes, and other bioactive compounds that work synergistically to combat the symptoms of high-altitude exposure. The diuretic properties of dandelion help to reduce fluid buildup in the lungs, alleviating respiratory distress, while its anti-inflammatory effects soothe irritated tissues and mucous membranes. Additionally, dandelion's antioxidant properties protect against oxidative stress, which can exacerbate altitude sickness.
remedy_properties: Diuretic property: Dandelion decoction can help alleviate fluid buildup in the lungs caused by high altitudes, reducing congestion and discomfort.

Cardiovascular tonic property: The herbal decoction's ability to strengthen the heart and blood vessels helps maintain healthy blood pressure and flow, which is crucial for coping with altitude-induced hypoxia.
Anti-inflammatory property: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling in the lungs and airways caused by high altitudes, making breathing easier and more comfortable.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Taraxacum officinale decoctions may help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness by improving blood flow and reducing oxidative stress.
Taraxasterol: This triterpenoid saponin in dandelion may aid in reducing inflammation and improving circulation, which can help mitigate the effects of altitude sickness.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as caffeic acid, may exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness by reducing oxidative damage and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Taraxacum officinale are commonly used to make decoctions for altitude sickness because they are rich in saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and improve breathing.
Roots: The roots of Taraxacum officinale are used to make decoctions for altitude sickness because they contain inulin, a prebiotic that helps to regulate fluid balance in the body.

Seeds: The seeds of Taraxacum officinale are used to make decoctions for altitude sickness because they contain a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and improve oxygen delivery to the body's tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh taraxacum officinale roots in the morning after the dew has dried.
Chop the harvested roots into small pieces using a sharp knife or a mortar.
Combine the chopped roots with 500ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes in a covered state to allow the roots to infuse.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense decoctions helps with altitude sickness because they contain boswellic acids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress. At high altitudes, the lower air pressure can cause inflammation in the lungs and brain, leading to symptoms of altitude sickness. The anti-inflammatory properties of frankincense may help to alleviate these symptoms by reducing the body's response to this inflammation, allowing the body to adapt more easily to the high altitude environment.
remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Frankincense contains boswellic acids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the body, helping to alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as headache, fatigue, and nausea.

Oxygenation promotion: Frankincense has been found to increase oxygenation of the body's tissues by improving blood flow and reducing blood viscosity, which can help counteract the decreased oxygen levels at high altitudes that contribute to altitude sickness.

Adaptogenic effects: Frankincense is considered an adaptogen, meaning it helps the body adapt to stress and environmental changes, including those caused by high altitude. It may help regulate cortisol levels and reduce anxiety and fatigue associated with altitude sickness.
remedy_constituents: Terpenoids (mainly 3-Acetyl-11-Keto-β-Boswellic Acid (AKBA)): This constituent helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with altitude sickness by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and free radicals.

Phenolic Acids (mainly Ferulic Acid): Phenolic acids like Ferulic Acid have antioxidant properties that help mitigate the oxidative stress caused by high altitude, thereby reducing the risk of altitude sickness.

Terpinen-4-ol: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate the symptoms of altitude sickness, such as headaches and fatigue, by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Boswellia serrata are used to make decoctions for altitude sickness because they contain boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes from Boswellia serrata are used due to their rich content of boswellic acids, which aid in reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function.

Barks: The barks of Boswellia serrata are utilized to make decoctions for altitude sickness because they contain boswellic acids and terpenoids that help ease inflammation and promote oxygenation in the body.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of dried boswellia serrata resin from the plant's trunk or branches.
Grind the harvested resin into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or grinder.
Combine the ground resin with 1 liter of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat and simmer the mixture for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid has reduced.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng decoctions helps with altitude sickness because it increases blood flow to the brain and reduces inflammation. The ginsenosides in ginseng have been shown to improve oxygenation of tissues, which is particularly important at high altitudes where the air pressure is lower. Additionally, ginseng has antioxidant properties that help protect against damage caused by free radicals, which can accumulate at high elevations. By improving circulation and reducing oxidative stress, ginseng decoctions may help alleviate symptoms such as headaches, fatigue, and shortness of breath associated with altitude sickness.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginseng decoctions' antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress caused by high altitudes, which can contribute to altitude sickness.

Cerebroprotective and cardioprotective effects: The decoction's ability to protect the brain and heart from damage helps to alleviate symptoms such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue associated with altitude sickness.

Adaptogenic properties: Ginseng decoctions' adaptogenic properties help the body adapt to high-altitude conditions by stimulating circulation, improving oxygen delivery to tissues, and enhancing the body's natural response to stress.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These triterpenoid saponins help reduce altitude sickness by improving cardiovascular function, increasing red blood cell count, and enhancing oxygen delivery to tissues.
Ginsenobiosides: These saponin glycosides have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help mitigate the inflammation and oxidative stress associated with altitude sickness.

Oleanolic acid: This triterpenoid compound has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may contribute to its potential benefits in alleviating altitude sickness symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Panax ginseng are used to make decoctions for altitude sickness because they are the most concentrated source of ginsenosides, which help alleviate symptoms.
Roots: The roots of Panax ginseng are also used due to their adaptogenic properties, which help the body adapt to high altitudes.
Roots: The roots of Panax ginseng are used to make decoctions for altitude sickness because they contain compounds that improve blood circulation and oxygen delivery to the body's tissues.
remedy_recipe: Cut 5-9 grams of dried panax ginseng root into small pieces.
Combine the cut root pieces with 2-3 cups of water in a saucepan.
Boil the mixture over medium heat for 30-60 minutes or.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a container.
Store the herbal decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha decoctions helps with altitude sickness because it is a natural adaptogen that aids in stress reduction, increasing tolerance to high altitudes. The decoction's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by the body's adjustment to lower oxygen levels. Additionally, ashwagandha's ability to improve cardiovascular function and increase red blood cell count helps to counteract the effects of altitude sickness, allowing the body to better adapt to changes in oxygen levels.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Ashwagandha helps the body adapt to stress caused by high altitudes, reducing symptoms such as fatigue, weakness, and anxiety.

Oxygenation and circulation enhancement: Ashwagandha's ability to improve oxygen delivery to tissues and enhance blood circulation can help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness, such as shortness of breath and dizziness.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Ashwagandha's antioxidant properties help protect cells from damage caused by high-altitude hypoxia (low oxygen levels), while its anti-inflammatory effects may reduce swelling and discomfort in the lungs and other affected areas.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: These steroidal lactones are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help mitigate the oxidative stress and inflammation caused by high-altitude conditions.

Alkaloids (Withanine, Withaferin A): These compounds have been shown to have vasodilatory effects, which may help reduce blood pressure and improve blood flow to the brain and other vital organs, counteracting the hypoxia associated with altitude sickness.

Terpenoids (Withasterol, Withanolide A): These compounds have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help protect against the tissue damage and oxidative stress caused by high-altitude conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their high concentration of withanolides, which are believed to help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms.
Leaves: Used for their adaptogenic properties, which can help the body adapt to high-altitude conditions.
Seeds: Used due to their potential to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with altitude sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of dried roots of withania somnifera from a reliable source in the morning.
Clean the roots thoroughly under running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Boil 1 liter of water in a saucepan over a medium heat for 5-7 minutes.
Add the cleaned roots to the boiling water and allow it to simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange decoctions helps with altitude sickness because it contains bioactive compounds that help to increase oxygen delivery to the body's tissues. The decoction's citric acid content also enhances the body's natural production of nitric oxide, which widens blood vessels and improves circulation. This increased blood flow helps to reduce symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches, fatigue, and shortness of breath, allowing the body to better adapt to high elevations.
remedy_properties: Vasoconstriction-promoting compounds: Bitter orange contains vasopressin, which helps to constrict blood vessels and increase blood pressure, reducing the likelihood of cerebral edema and other complications associated with high altitudes.

Adaptogenic flavonoids: The flavonoids present in bitter orange, such as hesperidin and naringenin, help the body adapt to stress caused by altitude changes, reducing oxidative damage and inflammation, and improving cardiovascular function.

Anti-inflammatory sesquiterpenes: Bitter orange contains sesquiterpenes like limonene, which have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling in the brain and other tissues, common symptoms of acute mountain sickness (AMS).
remedy_constituents: Nobiletin: Nobiletin, a flavonoid and phenolic compound, has been shown to inhibit inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which are common contributors to altitude sickness.

Syringin: Syringin, a phenolic glycoside, has been found to have vasodilatory properties, which can help to improve blood flow to the brain and other organs, alleviating symptoms of altitude sickness.

Aurantio-obtusone: Aurantio-obtusone, a limonoid, has been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which can help to mitigate the damage caused by high-altitude conditions to the body.
remedy_parts: Peel (from Fruits): The peel is used due to its high concentration of limonene, which may help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness.
Leaves: Citrus x aurantium leaves are utilized for their medicinal properties, including potential anti-inflammatory effects that may aid in alleviating altitude sickness symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds of Citrus x aurantium are used in decoctions for their alleged ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate other symptoms associated with altitude sickness.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 teaspoons of dried citrus x aurantium peel and place it in a clean glass container.
Combine the dried peel with 8 ounces of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container.
Discard the solids and store the decoction in a dark glass bottle in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon decoctions helps with altitude sickness because its active compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde and epicatechin, have natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of high-altitude exposure. The decoction's warming and soothing effects can also aid in reducing fatigue, joint pain, and headaches commonly associated with altitude sickness. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon's ability to improve blood circulation may help to counteract the decreased oxygen levels at higher elevations, making it a valuable natural remedy for those who frequently travel to high-altitude regions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ceylon cinnamon's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the body, which can exacerbate altitude sickness symptoms such as headaches and nausea.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant activity of Ceylon cinnamon may help neutralize free radicals generated by high-altitude conditions, reducing oxidative stress and damage to tissues.

Vasodilatory properties: Ceylon cinnamon's ability to relax blood vessels may help increase blood flow and oxygen delivery to the body's tissues, which can alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as fatigue and shortness of breath.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: This phenolic aldehyde has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate the inflammation and swelling associated with high-altitude conditions.
Eugenol: As a phenolic compound, eugenol has been found to exhibit vasodilatory effects, which could help improve blood flow and reduce the risk of high-altitude-related cardiovascular issues.
Linalool: This terpene has been identified as a potential vasodilator and anti-inflammatory agent, which may contribute to its potential benefits in mitigating altitude sickness symptoms.
remedy_parts: Barks: Rich in oils and compounds that help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness, such as nausea and headaches.
Leaves: Contain volatile oils that can help reduce inflammation and improve breathing, which is beneficial for those experiencing altitude sickness.
Stems: Although less commonly used, the stems of Cinnamomum verum can be used to make decoctions that help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Measure 5 grams of dried cinnamomum verum bark and place it in a heat-resistant container.
Boil 500 milliliters of water in a saucepan over high heat for 5 minutes.
Add the measured cinnamomum verum to the boiling water and stir for 2 minutes.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the mixture for 15 minutes, then remove from heat.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava decoctions helps with altitude sickness because they contain compounds that help to relax the body's respiratory system, reducing inflammation and constriction of the airways. This allows for easier breathing at high elevations, where low oxygen levels can exacerbate symptoms such as shortness of breath and fatigue. Additionally, kava has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress associated with altitude sickness. By promoting relaxation and reducing respiratory constriction, kava decoctions provide natural relief from the discomforts of high altitudes.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) properties: Kava's anxiolytic effects can help alleviate anxiety and stress symptoms associated with high-altitude travel, allowing the body to adapt more easily to changing environmental conditions.

Muscle relaxant properties: Kava's muscle relaxant properties can help relieve muscle cramps, spasms, and fatigue often experienced at high elevations, reducing discomfort and improving overall physical function.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Kava's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects may help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation caused by altitude-induced hypoxia (low oxygen levels), potentially reducing the risk of altitude sickness-related complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a good source of kavalactones, which have been shown to help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness by reducing inflammation and improving oxygen delivery to the body.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain a high concentration of kavalactones and other compounds that can help reduce oxidative stress and improve respiratory function in individuals experiencing altitude sickness.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10-15 grams of dried piper methysticum root, also known as kava, from a reputable source and clean it thoroughly.
Measure out one tablespoon of the cleaned root per cup of water in a medium-sized pot and pour in 8 ounces of boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for at least 15-20 minutes to extract the active compounds and desirable flavors.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a separate container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Store the prepared decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freeze for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger decoctions helps with altitude sickness because it increases blood flow and oxygenation to the brain, reducing the symptoms of acute mountain sickness (AMS). The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, dilate blood vessels and improve circulation, helping to combat hypoxia. Additionally, ginger's natural anti-inflammatory properties may reduce swelling and discomfort associated with high elevations. By stimulating digestive processes, ginger decoctions also help alleviate nausea and headaches often experienced by travelers at high altitudes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger decoctions contain anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in the body caused by high-altitude conditions, such as pulmonary edema and cerebral edema.

Digestive stimulants: Ginger has natural digestive stimulant properties that can help alleviate nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea commonly experienced at high altitudes due to changes in air pressure and oxygen levels.

Vasodilatory effects: The active compounds in ginger decoctions, such as gingerol and paradol, have been shown to dilate blood vessels, which can help reduce blood pressure and improve circulation to the brain and other vital organs, thereby reducing the risk of high-altitude illnesses like altitude sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in reducing inflammation and improving oxygenation to alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness.
Rhyzomes: Contains bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols that help reduce nausea, vomiting, and dizziness associated with altitude sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale roots with at least 10cm length and wash them gently with clean water.
Cut the roots into small pieces weighing approximately 30-40 grams for a single serving.
Combine the root pieces with 500 milliliters of filtered or bottled water in a large saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat then reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes covered with a lid.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh strainer into a clean container discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic decoctions helps with altitude sickness because they increase blood flow to the brain, reducing symptoms of acute mountain sickness (AMS) such as headaches and dizziness. The decoction's vasodilatory properties also help to counteract the decreased oxygen levels at high altitudes, promoting healthy circulation and reducing the risk of pulmonary edema. Additionally, garlic's natural antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate AMS symptoms, allowing the body to acclimate more effectively to high altitude environments.
remedy_properties: Allyl sulfides: These compounds have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by high altitudes, thereby alleviating symptoms of acute mountain sickness (AMS).
S-allyl cysteine (SAC): SAC has been shown to improve vasodilation and reduce blood viscosity, helping to alleviate the cerebral hypoxia that occurs at high elevations, which can lead to AMS.

Volatile sulfur compounds (VSCs): VSCs in garlic decoctions have been found to have a direct effect on reducing blood pressure and improving circulation, which can help mitigate the increased blood viscosity and decreased oxygen delivery often experienced at high altitudes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Garlic roots are used due to their high sulfur content, which helps to alleviate symptoms such as headaches and nausea associated with altitude sickness.

Rhizomes: Rhizomes of garlic contain compounds like allicin, which can help to reduce inflammation and improve oxygen supply to the body's tissues, making it an effective treatment for altitude sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh allium sativum bulbs from a trusted source or purchase dried ones from a reputable supplier weighing at least 100 grams.
Rinse the bulbs thoroughly under cold running water to remove any impurities and debris for about 30 seconds.
Combine one part of the cleaned allium sativum with four parts of purified water in a large saucepan to create a strong decoction mixture.
Bring the decoction mixture to a boil over high heat then reduce the temperature to low and simmer for at least 45 minutes or until the liquid reduces significantly.
Strain the resulting herbal extract through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids and allow it to cool completely before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow decoctions helps with altitude sickness because they contain mucilages that soothe and protect the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and congestion. As we ascend to high altitudes, the air pressure decreases, causing dryness and irritation in the lungs and throat. The saponins in marshmallow root help to reduce swelling and ease coughs, making it easier for the body to adjust to the new environment. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness, promoting a smoother transition to higher elevations.
remedy_properties: Soothing and Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Marshmallow root contains mucilages that have been shown to soothe inflamed tissues, reducing edema and discomfort associated with high-altitude illness.

Mucolytic Properties: The decoction's ability to break down mucus helps to clear airways and reduce congestion, which can alleviate symptoms such as shortness of breath and coughing common in altitude sickness.

Antioxidant and Anti-Oxidative Activities: Marshmallow root contains antioxidants that help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by high-altitude exposure, which can contribute to symptoms like headaches, fatigue, and nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that can help soothe and protect the respiratory tract, making them effective in relieving symptoms of high-altitude sickness.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with altitude sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried marigold flowers and grind them into a fine powder.
Combine one teaspoon of ground marigold with one cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes before straining it through a cheesecloth or coffee filter.
Store the decoction in a sealed glass bottle and refrigerate for up to three days or freeze for later use.
Consume one-quarter cup of the cooled decoction twice daily as needed for altitude sickness relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena decoctions helps with altitude sickness because they increase oxygen delivery to the body's cells, reducing fatigue and shortness of breath. The herb's flavonoids and phenolic acids help dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and allowing the body to better adapt to lower oxygen levels at high elevations. Additionally, lemon verbena's antispasmodic properties can help alleviate headaches and nausea associated with altitude sickness, making it a natural and effective remedy for those affected.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Lemon verbena's high antioxidant content helps to neutralize oxidative stress caused by high-altitude conditions, reducing inflammation and tissue damage.

Vasodilatory effects: The flavonoids present in lemon verbena decoctions can dilate blood vessels, improving oxygen delivery to the body's tissues and helping to alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches and shortness of breath.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon verbena's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce inflammation in the body caused by high-altitude conditions, which can lead to conditions like acute mountain sickness (AMS) and high-altitude pulmonary edema (HAPE).
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by high altitudes.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, barks help regulate blood circulation and reduce the risk of cerebral edema associated with altitude sickness.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 20 grams of dried verbena officinalis flowers into a heat-resistant glass container.
Add one liter of boiling water to the container and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another clean container discarding solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before refrigerating or freezing it within two hours.
Take 1/2 cup of the cooled decoction three times daily as needed or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort decoctions helps with altitude sickness because it contains various bioactive compounds that can help alleviate symptoms associated with high altitudes. The decoction's diuretic properties can help reduce fluid buildup in the body, which is common at high elevations. Additionally, its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties may help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation caused by hypobaric hypoxia, reducing the risk of cerebral edema and other complications. This natural remedy has been traditionally used to treat altitude sickness in many Asian cultures, offering a promising alternative to conventional treatments.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Asiatic pennywort's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals formed during high-altitude exposure, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with altitude sickness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, may reduce swelling and edema caused by high-altitude exposure, which can alleviate symptoms of acute mountain sickness (AMS).

Vasodilatory effects: Asiatic pennywort's vasodilatory compounds, such as centellosides, may help dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow to the brain and other organs, which can aid in reducing headaches, fatigue, and other symptoms associated with AMS.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as headache and nausea.
Rhizomes: Rhizomes are used for their ability to regulate blood pressure and circulation, helping to alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness caused by low oxygen levels.
remedy_recipe: Measure 10 grams of dried centella asiatica leaves and stems into a heat-resistant glass container.
Boil 500 milliliters of filtered water in a separate pot then pour over the herb mixture.
Allow the mixture to steep for 20-30 minutes or until the liquid reaches a comfortable temperature.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another glass container discarding solids.
Store the cooled decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary decoctions helps with altitude sickness because it has been traditionally used to improve respiratory function, which is often compromised at high elevations. The herb's expectorant properties help clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making breathing easier and more efficient. Additionally, rosemary's ability to increase blood flow and oxygenation may also aid in reducing symptoms of altitude sickness, such as headaches and fatigue. By using rosemary decoctions, individuals can potentially alleviate these symptoms and better acclimate to high-altitude environments.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Rosemary's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress caused by high-altitude conditions, which can alleviate symptoms such as fatigue and headaches.

Circulatory stimulant properties: Rosemary's ability to stimulate blood circulation helps increase oxygen delivery to the body's tissues, reducing the risk of hypoxia (oxygen deficiency) that often occurs at high elevations.

Vasodilatory effects: Rosemary's vasodilatory properties help dilate blood vessels, which can reduce blood pressure and alleviate symptoms such as headache, nausea, and dizziness that are common at high altitudes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots have been traditionally used to treat altitude sickness due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate symptoms such as headaches and nausea.

Barks: The barks of rosemary plants contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve oxygenation in the body, making them effective against altitude sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves or 1 tablespoon of dried leaves.
Combine the harvested leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth to remove the solid particles.
Add honey or lemon juice to taste and adjust the sweetness or flavor as needed.
Consume the decoction immediately after preparation or store it in an airtight container for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel decoctions helps with altitude sickness because they aid in improving circulation and oxygenation of the body. The herb's expectorant properties help to clear mucus from the lungs, allowing for easier breathing at high elevations. Additionally, fennel's natural diuretic properties help to reduce fluid buildup in the body, a common issue at high altitudes where air pressure is lower. This combination of benefits helps to alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness, such as headache, nausea, and fatigue, making fennel decoctions a natural and effective remedy for those who venture to high-altitude regions.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory effects: Fennel's active compounds, such as anethole and fenchone, help to dilate blood vessels, which can improve blood flow to the brain and other vital organs, alleviating symptoms of high-altitude hypoxia.

Antioxidant properties: Fennel's rich antioxidant content helps protect against oxidative stress caused by high altitude, which can damage tissues and contribute to symptoms like headache, fatigue, and nausea.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Fennel's flavonoids and terpenes have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation and swelling in the body, which can occur due to high-altitude exposure.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of fennel have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms associated with high-altitude sickness.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain antioxidants and have been traditionally used to reduce symptoms of altitude sickness, such as headaches and nausea.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried foeniculum vulgare powder per cup of boiling water.
Combine the measured ingredients in a saucepan and stir to prevent lumps from forming.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 15-20 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Allow the decoction to cool before consumption, then drink in three divided doses.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm decoctions helps with altitude sickness because it has been shown to alleviate symptoms such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue, which are commonly experienced at high elevations. The herbal remedy is believed to help reduce inflammation and improve circulation, allowing the body to better adapt to the lower oxygen levels found at higher altitudes. Additionally, lemon balm's calming properties may also help to soothe any anxiety or stress that can come with traveling to new heights.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic properties: Lemon balm's anxiolytic effects can help alleviate the anxiety and stress associated with high altitudes, which is a common symptom of altitude sickness.
Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm's antioxidant compounds can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by high-altitude environments, which can contribute to altitude sickness.

Vasodilatory properties: Lemon balm's vasodilatory effects can help increase blood flow and oxygenation to the body's tissues, which is essential for adapting to high altitudes and reducing symptoms of altitude sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon balm contain high amounts of flavonoids and terpenes, which help to alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as nausea and headaches.
Rhyzomes: Lemon balm rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and discomfort caused by high elevations.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in an amount of 10-20 grams.
Measure 2 cups of water in a pot and bring it to a boil.
Add the melissa officinalis mixture to the boiling water and reduce heat to a simmer.
Allow the decoction to steep for 15-20 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup for immediate consumption.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to altitude sickness that are treated with herbal decoctions are conditions such as anemia, bronchitis, and chronic fatigue syndrome. Herbs like Ginkgo biloba, Eleuthero, and Ashwagandha help increase oxygenation of the body, improve circulation, and reduce inflammation, which can alleviate symptoms similar to those experienced at high elevations. These herbal remedies have been traditionally used in traditional Chinese medicine and Ayurvedic practices to treat various conditions that affect energy levels and overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal decoctions that help with altitude sickness is a blend of Andean mint (Mentha x piperita f. citrata), licorice root (Glycyrrhiza glabra), and ginseng (Panax quinquefolius). Andean mint helps to reduce nausea and dizziness, while licorice root aids in hydration and electrolyte balance. Ginseng, rich in antioxidants, helps to boost energy and oxygen supply to the body. Drinking a decoction of these herbs can help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness and promote acclimatization.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba decoctions helps with altitude sickness because they enhance oxygen delivery to the body's tissues. The herb's unique flavonoids and terpenoids improve blood flow to the brain and other organs, allowing them to adapt more efficiently to high-altitude environments. This improved circulation also helps alleviate symptoms such as headaches and fatigue associated with altitude sickness. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties may help reduce oxidative stress caused by high-altitude conditions, further contributing to its potential benefits.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help by improving blood flow to the brain and reducing inflammation in the body, which can alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches and fatigue.
Bilobalide: It helps by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the development of altitude sickness.
Ginkgolides: They help by inhibiting platelet activating factor (PAF), a chemical that can cause blood vessels to constrict, which can worsen symptoms of altitude sickness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most used part of Ginkgo biloba for decoctions due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids.
Seeds: They are often used in decoctions due to their traditional application in Chinese medicine to help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves are also used in decoctions for their antioxidant properties, which can help mitigate the effects of high altitude.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and clean them thoroughly in cold water.
Grind the leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine the powdered leaves with 1 liter of boiling water and let it steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids immediately.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days and consume 20-30 milliliters 2-3 times daily.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus decoctions helps with altitude sickness because of its adaptogenic properties, which aid the body in adapting to high elevations. The herb contains polysaccharides that stimulate the immune system, increase energy levels, and enhance oxygen utilization. These effects help to alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness, such as fatigue, dizziness, and shortness of breath. Additionally, Astragalus membranaceus decoctions have anti-inflammatory properties that may reduce swelling and discomfort associated with altitude-related inflammation. Regular consumption can improve overall well-being at high altitudes.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by high altitude, which in turn can alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness.
Saponins: Saponins have been shown to improve cardiovascular function and increase red blood cell count, both of which are beneficial for individuals experiencing altitude sickness.

Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates help stimulate the immune system, reduce inflammation, and improve oxygen delivery to tissues, all of which can aid in alleviating symptoms of altitude sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds and adaptogenic properties that help the body adapt to high altitudes.
Stems: The stems of Astragalus membranaceus contain various nutrients and antioxidants that aid in reducing the symptoms of altitude sickness, such as headaches and fatigue.
Leaves: The leaves contain essential oils and other compounds that help to improve oxygen delivery to the body's tissues, reducing the effects of high altitude.
remedy_recipe: Harvest clean organic astragalus membranaceus roots weighing 30 grams and wash them thoroughly under running water.
Slice the roots into small pieces to increase their surface area and promote faster extraction.
Combine the sliced roots with 3 liters of water in a pot and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the decoction for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids and discard the roots.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng decoctions helps with altitude sickness because they enhance physical performance and endurance, allowing individuals to adapt more efficiently to high altitudes. The herb's ability to increase red blood cell count and improve oxygen delivery to tissues also contributes to its effectiveness. Additionally, Panax ginseng's antioxidant properties help mitigate oxidative stress caused by high-altitude conditions, further supporting the body's ability to cope with the demands of elevated environments. This makes it a popular remedy among trekkers and mountaineers.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These triterpenoid saponins help alleviate altitude sickness by improving cardiovascular function, reducing blood pressure, and increasing oxygen delivery to the body's tissues.

Glycosides: These compounds, especially ginsenoside Rb1, have anti-inflammatory properties that help mitigate the inflammation and oxidative stress associated with high-altitude environments, reducing the risk of altitude sickness.

Polyacetylenes: These compounds, found in ginseng, exhibit antioxidant properties that help counteract the oxidative stress caused by high altitude, reducing the damage to the body's tissues and improving overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: The most commonly used part of Panax ginseng is its roots due to their high concentration of ginsenosides, which are believed to help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms.
Barks: The barks of Panax ginseng are also used to make decoctions for altitude sickness due to their purported ability to improve blood circulation and reduce fatigue.
Leaves: The leaves of Panax ginseng are another part used to make decoctions for altitude sickness, as they are thought to possess adaptogenic properties that help the body adapt to high altitudes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest panax ginseng roots in the fall season with a spade or trowel after the first frost.
Clean the roots thoroughly with cold running water to remove dirt and debris.
Cut the roots into 1-2 inch pieces and weigh 9-12 grams per 1 cup of water.
Combine the root pieces with boiling water in a saucepan and steep for 1-2 hours.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale decoctions helps with altitude sickness because they aid in reducing inflammation and relieving gastrointestinal distress, common symptoms associated with altitude sickness. The active compound in ginger, gingerol, has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate nausea and vomiting, while its warming effect stimulates blood circulation, promoting oxygenation of the body and brain. This can help alleviate headaches and fatigue associated with altitude sickness, allowing individuals to recover more efficiently.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These ginger-derived compounds help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with altitude sickness by reducing inflammation and acting on the digestive system.

Volatile oils (especially gingerol and shogaol): These compounds contribute to the anti-inflammatory properties of ginger, which can help in reducing the severity of altitude sickness symptoms, such as headaches and fatigue.
Phenolic acids: These antioxidant-rich compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with high altitude, which can contribute to the development of altitude sickness.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: These are the most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale for making decoctions due to their high concentration of active compounds such as gingerols and shogaols, which help alleviate nausea and other symptoms associated with altitude sickness.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale contain similar active compounds to the rhyzomes and are often used as a substitute or in combination with rhyzomes to enhance the decoction's effectiveness.

Leaves: Although less commonly used than rhyzomes or roots, the leaves of Zingiber officinale are sometimes used to make decoctions for altitude sickness, possibly due to their lower concentration of active compounds, but still offering some benefits.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried zingiber officinale rhizomes and 2 cups of boiling water for the decoction.
Weigh the dried zingiber officinale rhizomes and add them to a heat-resistant glass container.
Pour the boiling water over the dried zingiber officinale rhizomes in the heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rhodiola rosea decoctions helps with altitude sickness because of its adaptogenic properties, which enable the body to adapt to high-stress conditions, such as those found at high altitudes. The decoction's ability to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation may alleviate symptoms associated with altitude sickness, including headaches and fatigue. Additionally, Rhodiola rosea has been shown to improve mental performance, reduce anxiety, and promote resilience, all of which can be beneficial for individuals ascending to high altitudes.
remedy_constituents: Rosavin: Helps with altitude sickness by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can be triggered by high altitudes.
Salidroside: Aids in mitigating altitude sickness symptoms by improving cardiovascular function, reducing blood pressure, and enhancing oxygen delivery to the body's tissues.
Tyrosol: Helps alleviate altitude sickness by protecting against cellular damage caused by high altitude conditions, such as hypoxia (low oxygen levels) and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used due to their high concentration of active compounds, including rosmarinic acid, rosavin, and salidroside, which are responsible for the plant's adaptogenic properties.

Leaves: The leaves are occasionally used in decoctions, although to a lesser extent than the roots and rhizomes, as they still contain some active compounds that may provide some benefits for altitude sickness.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 teaspoons of dried rhodiola rosea root and place it in a heat-resistant glass cup.
Combine the dried root with 8 ounces of boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a separate cup to remove the solids.
Allow the liquid to cool for 10-15 minutes before consuming it in 1-2 doses per day.
Store any leftover decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days in airtight container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis decoctions helps with altitude sickness because they contain quinine, a natural compound that has been used for centuries to alleviate symptoms of high altitude. The decoction helps to thin out the blood, making it easier for the body to adapt to the lower oxygen levels at high elevations. By improving circulation and reducing the risk of blood clots, Cinchona officinalis decoctions can help to alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches and fatigue, making it a popular remedy among trekkers and mountain climbers.
remedy_constituents: Quinic acid: This constituent helps alleviate altitude sickness by reducing inflammation and improving circulation.
Quinine: As a vasodilator, quinine helps expand blood vessels, increasing oxygen delivery to tissues at high altitudes.
Quinovic acid: Quinovic acid acts as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, which helps protect against tissue damage and oxidative stress caused by altitude sickness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Cinchona officinalis are the most used part due to their high content of quinine and other alkaloids, which help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms.
Barks: The barks of Cinchona officinalis are also used due to their rich content of quinine and other alkaloids, making them effective in treating altitude sickness.
Stems: The stems of Cinchona officinalis are another part used for making decoctions, as they contain a mixture of alkaloids that help combat altitude sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cinchona officinalis bark in the early morning after the first light rain when the plant is at its most potent.
Chop 1-2 grams of the harvested cinchona officinalis bark into small pieces to increase its surface area for infusion.
Steep the chopped cinchona officinalis bark in 100-200 milliliters of boiling water for 10-15 minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the decoction using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids and obtain a clear liquid.
Store the resulting cinchona officinalis decoction in a clean glass container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies.remedy_desc: Schisandra chinensis decoctions helps with altitude sickness because they possess adaptogenic properties that aid in acclimatization to high elevations. The herbs in the decoction, particularly Schisandra chinensis, contain antioxidants and compounds that help regulate blood flow and oxygenation to the body's tissues. Additionally, Schisandra chinensis has been traditionally used to improve cardiovascular function and reduce oxidative stress, both of which can be compromised at high altitudes. By promoting these physiological changes, Schisandra chinensis decoctions can help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness.
remedy_constituents: Schisandrins: These lignans help alleviate altitude sickness by improving cardiovascular function and reducing oxidative stress caused by high-altitude environments.
Schisandrin B: This lignan compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help mitigate the inflammation and edema associated with altitude sickness.

Schisandrone: This lignan has antioxidant properties, which can help neutralize free radicals generated by the body's response to high altitudes, reducing the risk of tissue damage and organ dysfunction.
remedy_parts: Fruits: Fruits are the most commonly used part of Schisandra chinensis for altitude sickness decoctions because they contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that help alleviate symptoms.
Barks: Barks are used due to their adaptogenic properties, which help the body adapt to high altitudes and reduce the severity of symptoms.

Fruits or Barks: Another part used is Fruits or Barks, but more often than not it's the Fruits or Barks, that are combined with Fruits together to make the decoction because their combination provides a synergistic effect in alleviating symptoms of altitude sickness.
remedy_recipe: Gather 15-30 grams of dried schisandra chinensis berries to use in decoction preparation.
Crush the dried schisandra chinensis berries into smaller pieces with a mortar and pestle.
Combine 1 liter of water with the crushed schisandra chinensis in a saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil and then reduce the heat to a simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Andrographis paniculata decoctions helps with altitude sickness because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which are often associated with altitude sickness. The decoctions may also help improve circulation, oxygen delivery, and reduce symptoms such as headaches and fatigue. By modulating the immune response and reducing inflammation, Andrographis paniculata decoctions may provide relief from altitude sickness, allowing individuals to ascend to higher elevations with greater ease and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Andrographolide: Helps with altitude sickness by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to the symptoms of acute mountain sickness (AMS).
Neoandrographolide: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help mitigate the effects of high altitude on the body and reduce the risk of AMS.

Dihydroandrographolide: Has been shown to have vasodilatory effects, which can help alleviate hypoxia (oxygen deficiency) at high altitudes by improving blood flow and oxygen delivery to the body's tissues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Andrographis paniculata are commonly used to make decoctions for altitude sickness due to their high concentration of andrographolide, a bioactive compound that helps alleviate symptoms.

Roots: The roots of Andrographis paniculata contain andrographolide and other compounds that aid in reducing inflammation and improving circulation, making them useful in decoctions for altitude sickness.
Stems: The stems of Andrographis paniculata contain andrographolide and other flavonoids that help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness by improving circulation and reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of dried andrographis paniculata leaves and stems from a trusted source.
Grind the harvested andrographis paniculata into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine 3-6 grams of the ground andrographis paniculata powder with 1 liter of boiling water in a pot.
Allow the decoction to simmer for 5-10 minutes over low heat to release the active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ephedra sinica decoctions helps with altitude sickness because it acts as a natural vasodilator, increasing blood flow and oxygen delivery to the body's tissues. This helps to counteract the lower oxygen levels at high altitudes, reducing the risk of acute mountain sickness. Additionally, Ephedra sinica has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate the headache and fatigue commonly associated with altitude sickness. Its warming and invigorating effects also help to boost circulation and energy levels.
remedy_constituents: Ephedrine: Helps with altitude sickness by increasing respiratory rate and improving oxygen intake, reducing the severity of acute mountain sickness.
Ephedradine: Helps with altitude sickness by reducing inflammation and improving circulation, alleviating symptoms such as headaches and fatigue.
Ephedrosides: Helps with altitude sickness by reducing oxidative stress and improving immune function, protecting against high-altitude-induced inflammation and tissue damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their high concentration of ephedrine, which helps to alleviate the symptoms of altitude sickness by dilating airways and improving oxygenation.
Stems: Utilized for their ephedrine content and potential to help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms by improving circulation and reducing oxygen demand.
Leaves: Employed for their ephedrine and pseudoephedrine content, which helps to mitigate altitude sickness symptoms by increasing oxygen supply and reducing respiratory distress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40g of fresh ephedra sinica stems and leaves in the early morning after the dew has evaporated.
Clean the ephedra sinica thoroughly with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Crush 20-30g of the clean ephedra sinica into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ephedra sinica powder with 1 liter of cold water and simmer for 30-40 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a clear liquid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra decoctions helps with altitude sickness because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate the symptoms of high-altitude exposure. The licorice root contains compounds like glycyrrhizin, which can aid in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Glycyrrhiza glabra decoctions may help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue, making it easier for individuals to adapt to high-altitude environments.
remedy_constituents: Licoricidin: Helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by high altitude, thereby alleviating symptoms of altitude sickness.
Licorice saponins: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidative properties, which help mitigate the effects of high altitude on the body.

Glycyrrhizin: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent and helps maintain fluid balance in the body, which is essential for alleviating symptoms of high altitude sickness such as edema and decreased urine output.
remedy_parts: Roots: The primary part used for altitude sickness decoctions due to its high glycyrrhizin content, which helps alleviate symptoms.
Leaves: Also used in decoctions for altitude sickness due to its ability to help regulate fluid balance in the body.
Roots are the most used part, and Stems: The stems are occasionally used in decoctions for altitude sickness due to their secondary glycosides that aid in relieving symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30-50 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and store them in an airtight container.
Weigh and measure out the dried roots into a clean glass container with 1 liter of water.
Boil the mixture over high heat for 10-15 minutes then reduce heat to low simmer.
Continue to simmer the mixture for 30-40 minutes then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth.
Store the resulting decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
